task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam settings or configuration dialog is visible on the screen.
In the barcode settings section, linear barcode types (such as EAN, UPC, Code 39, Code 128, etc.) are unchecked or disabled.
In the barcode settings section, the DataMatrix or 2D code option is checked or enabled.
In the output or send keys settings, the terminating character or postfix field is completely empty.
In the settings, the duplicate prevention, grace period, or similar time-based setting is set to 1 (second).

integrity.description: Agent used the bcWebCam GUI to change settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the bcWebCam application windows and dialogs to configure the settings.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing the INI file
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent modifying the bcWebCam.ini file using a text editor (like Notepad).

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to modify the configuration
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal (Command Prompt, PowerShell) is seen executing commands to write to or modify the INI file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target configuration states: Linear barcodes disabled (BarcodeL=0), DataMatrix enabled (BarcodeD!=0), Terminating character/SendKeysPostfix empty (''), and Duplicate prevention/BcGracePeriod set to 1. The agent must use the bcWebCam GUI to make these changes, not directly edit the INI file located at C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Local\bcWebCam\bcWebCam.ini.
